title: System and method for remote property management
abstract: A system for use in a residential property, the system including a first computing system positionable in the residential property and including a first memory and a first processor. The system also includes a second computing system including a second memory and a second processor, the second computing system in communication with the first computing system over a network. The first memory comprises program instructions executable by the processor of the first computing system to: recognize a sound indicative of an unexpected event; retrieve, from a database stored in the first memory, an acoustic profile of the unexpected event; and responsive to the identifying the unexpected event, transmit a notification including an identity of the acoustic profile of the unexpected event to the second computing system over the network.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A system for use in a residential property, the system comprising: a first computing system positionable in the residential property and including a first memory and a first processor; a second computing system including a second memory and a second processor, the second computing system in communication with the first computing system over a network; wherein the first memory comprises program instructions executable by the processor of the first computing system to: recognize a sound indicative of an unexpected event; retrieve, from a database stored in the first memory, an acoustic profile of the unexpected event; and responsive to retrieving the identity of the unexpected event, transmit a notification including an identity of the acoustic profile of the unexpected event to the second computing system over the network. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein the notification is transmitted in real-time. 3. The system of claim 1, wherein the notification includes a location of the unexpected event in the residential property. 4 The system of claim 1, wherein the notification is stored in the memory of the second computing device and is accessed when the second computing device is positioned in the residential property. 5. The system of claim 1, wherein the notification includes instructions for responding to the unexpected event. 6. The system of claim 1, further comprising a third computing system including a third processor and a third memory, the third computing system in communication with at least one of the first computing system and the second computing system over the network, and wherein at least one of the first memory and the second memory comprises program instructions executable by one of the first processor and the second processor to: responsive identifying the acoustic profile of the unexpected event, transmit a notification including the identity of the acoustic profile of the unexpected event to the third computing system over the network. 7. The system of claim 6, further comprising an electronic lock controlling access to the residential property, and wherein the notification includes a passcode to the electronic lock. 8. The system of claim 7, wherein the passcode to the electronic lock is unique to the unexpected event. 9. The system of claim 6, wherein the notification prompts an operator of the third computing device to transmit confirmation that the operator has responded to the unexpected event to one of the first computing device and the second computing device over the network. 10. A computer-implemented method comprising: sensing an unexpected event in a residential property, the unexpected event being one of a sound, a temperature, and a motion; responsive to sensing the unexpected event, querying a database stored on a server to retrieve a type of the unexpected event; responsive to the unexpected event corresponding to a trigger event, retrieving an access code for a lock controlling access to the residential property; and transmitting a notification of the trigger event and the access code to a user, the access code unique to the trigger event. 11. The computer-implemented method of claim 10, further comprising the step of storing a record of the unexpected event in a memory of a computing device positioned in the residential property. 12. The computer-implemented method of claim 11, further comprising the step of notifying the user to access the record of the unexpected event when the user is inside the residential property. 13. The computer-implemented method of claim 10, wherein the notification of the trigger event includes a location of the trigger event within the residential property. 14. The computer-implemented method of claim 10, wherein the notification includes instructions for responding to the trigger event. 15. The computer-implemented method of claim 10, further comprising the steps of transmitting a second notification of the trigger event to a second user, and requiring the first user to transmit confirmation of a response to the trigger event to the second user. 16. A computer-implemented method comprising: sensing an unexpected event in a residential property, the unexpected event being one of a sound, a temperature, and a motion; responsive to sensing the unexpected event, querying a database stored on a server to retrieve a type of the unexpected event; transmitting a notification of the unexpected event and the type of the unexpected event to a user; and prompting the user to transmit a confirmation of a response to the unexpected event to a second user. 17. The computer-implemented method of claim 16, further comprising the step of storing a record of the unexpected event in a computing device positioned in the residential property. 18. The computer-implemented method of claim 16, further comprising the step of notifying the user to access the record of the unexpected event when the user is inside the residential property. 19. The computer-implemented method of claim 16, wherein the notification of the unexpected event includes a location of the unexpected event within the residential property. 20. The computer-implemented method of claim 16, wherein the notification includes instructions for responding to the unexpected event.
